rulings.Case Title: CHEUK TIN YAN VS. LI MING LEI et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn For Order Compelling Defts To Attend And Testify At Depo And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, October 22, 2010, line 2. PLAINTIFF CHEUK YAN's Motion For Order Compelling Defendants To Attend And Testify At Deposition And For Monetary Sanctions is denied. As to Defendant Fu, Plaintiff failed to provide sufficient notice per CCP 1005. The motion is untimely and the earliest it could be heard by mail service was October 25, 2010. The motion is denied as to defendant Lei. Plaintiff fails to demonstrate good faith meet and confer efforts. Lei's counsel offered to present her for deposition. Parties to meet and confer to discuss new deposition date. Deny sanctions. = (302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN LUM ARCHITECTURE, INC VS. ARGYLE INVESTMENT PARTNERS, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, October 22, 2010, line 6, DEFENDANTS ASIA SF, LLC, GREGORY LOCKAMY, WILBUR YOUNG, LARRY HASHBARGER'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED IN PART AND OVERRULED IN PART. DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO 1ST CAUSE OF ACTION. PLAINTIFF MUST PLEAD EXHAUSTION OF THE MEDIATION/ ARBITRATION REMEDIES IN THE WRITTEN CONTRACTS, OR SPECIFIC FACTUAL ALLEGATIONS SHOWING WAIVER. SEE CAREAU & CO. V. SECURITY PAC. BUSINESS CREDIT, INC. (1990) 222 CAL. APP. 3D 1371. PLAINTIFF MUST ALSO PLEAD WHICH DEFENDANTS ARE LIABLE UNDER EACH CONTRACT AND WHAT DAMAGES ARE DUE UNDER EACH CONTRACT. DEMURRER OVERRULED AS TO 2ND, 3RD AND 4TH CAUSES OF ACTION. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: MARILYN GO VS. WACHOVIA MORTGAGE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, October 22, 2010, line 7, DEFENDANT WACHOVIA MORTGAGE, A DIVISION OF WELLS FARGO BANK, DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. AS TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF MUST SEPARATE THE BREACH OF CONTRACT AND VIOLATION OF C.C. SECTION 2923.5 CLAIMS INTO 2 CAUSES OF ACTION. THE ALLEGED ORAL AGREEMENT IS BARRED BY THE DEED OF TRUST THAT STATES THAT ALL MODIFICATION AGREEMENTS MUST BE REDUCED TO WRITING. THE C.C. SECTION 2923.5 CLAIM DOES NOT ALLEGE THAT DEFENDANTS DID NOT CONTACT PLAINTIFF PRIOR TO THE NOTICE OF DEFAULT, AND THE COMPLAINT ALLEGES CONTACT WAS MADE. THERE IS NOTHING IN C.C. SECTION 2923.5 THAT REQUIRES THE LENDER TO REWRITE OR MODIFY THE LOAN. SEE MABRY V. SUPERIOR COURT (2010) 185 CAL. APP. 4TH 208. AS TO THE 2ND, 3RD AND 5TH CAUSES OF ACTION, THE CLAIMS MUST BE PLED WITH PARTICULARITY. PLAINTIFF DOES NOT ADDRESS THE FACT THAT THERE WAS NO ASSIGNMENT OR SALE, BUT ONLY A CHANGE IN DEFENDANT'S NAME WHICH IS PROPERLY JUDICIALLY NOTICED. PLAINTIFF MUST STATE FACTS SUPPORTING RELIANCE AND RESULTING DAMAGES. ALSO, AS TO THE 5TH CAUSE OF ACTION INSUFFICIENT FACTS ARE PLEAD THAT THE AGREEMENT IS VOID AND DEFENDANT, AS A LENDER, OWES NO DUTY TO DISCLOSE THAT PLAINTIFF COULD NOT HAVE QUALIFIED FOR THE LOAN. AS TO THE 4TH CAUSE OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF DOES NOT ADDRESS DEFENDANT'S AUTHORITIES THAT THERE IS NO PRIVATE RIGHT OF ACTION UNDER THE STATUTE AND THERE IS NO DUTY TO OFFER A LOAN MODIFICATION. PLAINTIFF HAS NOT ALLEGED TENDER TO SUPPORT INJUNCTIVE RELIEF OR THE REQUEST TO QUIET TITLE. THE 6TH CAUSE OF ACTION DOES NOT ALLEGE SPECIFIC FACTS DETAILING THE ALLEGED CONTROVERSY. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: FERGUSON ENTERPRISES, INC. VS. RICHLYNN CONTRACTING COMPANY INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, October 22, 2010, line 8. CROSS DEFENDANTS FERGUSON ENTERPRISES, INC., KAREN ALARCON, RUBY URIBE, JOY FIELDS'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT. Sustained with 10 days leave to amend. Cross-complainant to plead the basis of each claim with more clarity and comply with California Rule of Court 2.112. Cross-complainant must set forth the party against whom a cause of action is alleged and describe the damages being requested with respect to each cause of action. The cross-complaint should contain a prayer at the end. =(302/CWW)
